---
title: "Intro to Bonds"
date: 06/07/2026
date-format: long
---

### Bond
A bond is a debt issued by issuer/debtor/borrower which is repaid to investor/lender the original amount borrowed + interest over specified period of time.\
A typical bond has a *`fixed date when the amount borrowed is due (principal issued is paid back) aka maturity date`*  and *`contractual amount of interest which is paid every six months`*

### Sectors of U.S. Bond Market
U.S. Fixed income market is divided into six sectors:\
* _U.S. Treasury_ : Includes securities issued by U.S. Government. These include Treasury Bills, Notes and Bonds. U.S. Govt securities set the tone for rest of the economy and world\
* _Agency_ : Securities issued by federally related institutes and government sponsored entities (GSE). These are not backed by collateral and also called as *_agency debenture securities_*\
* _Municipals_ : These bonds are issued by state and local governments. There are two types of bonds based on how the interest received by investors is taxed at federal income tax level. There are two types of bond structures:\
    + Tax Backed Bonds\
    + Revenue Bonds
* _Corporates_ : These include securities issued by U.S. Corporations and securities issued in U.S. by non U.S. Corporations. Corporations can issue *bonds, medium term notes and commercial paper*. Corporations can also borrow from commercial banks and sometimes these commercial loans can be traded in the market. Based on credit rating of corporations the securities are further classified into investment-grade and non investment-grade\
* _Asset Backed Securities_ : \
* _Mortgages_ : \
